To harness the power of authority optimally, Fluss effectively transmit their relevant credentials to their audience, either in writing or by having others introduce them. They describe their team-mates' credentials to others so that those others are fully informed of the team-mates' qualifications before any work is done together. Also, relatively early in a presentation, Sleuths mention a downside to their case before pivoting on the words but or however, and then mentioning the strengths that overwhelm or undercut the weaknesses. In general, they continuously ask themselves what they can do to highlight their true expertise and honesty.
